title: Data supporting: Low-Code Tool for Phytosociological Data Management and Analysis: Application of the Point-Centered Quarter Method via AppSheet
author: Giacomini, Ricardo Marostica
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset provides the foundational data supporting the methodological validation presented in the article   "Low-Code Tool for Phytosociological Data Management and Analysis: Application of the Point-Centered Quarter Method via AppSheet"  .  
  The repository includes three primary files:  
 
  Raw Field Collection Matrix (  Levantamento pontos quadrantes.csv  ):   Contains raw data collected in the field using the   Point-Centered Quarter Method (PCQM)  , including taxonomic identification, point-to-plant distances, and individualized circumference at breast height (CBH) for multiple stems  .  
  Processed Calculation Matrices (  fitossociologia.csv  ):   Displays the automated aggregation and calculation of structural descriptors, such as the   Importance Value Index (      I  V  I      )  , including relative density, frequency, and dominance  .  
  Ecological Indices Summary (  Resumo indices.csv  ):   Provides a synthesis of alpha-diversity metrics, including   Species Richness (      S      )  ,   Shannon-Wiener Index (       H        ′             )  , and   Pielou’s Equitability (       J        ′             )  , both for the total community and segmented by subcommunities (Area 1, Area 2, and Area 3)  .  
 
  These data allow for the verification of the system's logic and the replicability of the comparative analyses and sampling sufficiency monitoring (Species Accumulation Curve) discussed in the study  . The architecture demonstrates how a low-code platform can effectively eliminate manual transcription bottlenecks while maintaining taxonomic precision and data integrit.

This dataset provides the foundational data supporting the methodological validation presented in the article   "Low-Code Tool for Phytosociological Data Management and Analysis: Application of the Point-Centered Quarter Method via AppSheet"  .  
  The repository includes three primary files:  
 
  Raw Field Collection Matrix (  Levantamento pontos quadrantes.csv  ):   Contains raw data collected in the field using the   Point-Centered Quarter Method (PCQM)  , including taxonomic identification, point-to-plant distances, and individualized circumference at breast height (CBH) for multiple stems  .  
  Processed Calculation Matrices (  fitossociologia.csv  ):   Displays the automated aggregation and calculation of structural descriptors, such as the   Importance Value Index (      I  V  I      )  , including relative density, frequency, and dominance  .  
  Ecological Indices Summary (  Resumo indices.csv  ):   Provides a synthesis of alpha-diversity metrics, including   Species Richness (      S      )  ,   Shannon-Wiener Index (       H        ′             )  , and   Pielou’s Equitability (       J        ′             )  , both for the total community and segmented by subcommunities (Area 1, Area 2, and Area 3)  .  
 
  These data allow for the verification of the system's logic and the replicability of the comparative analyses and sampling sufficiency monitoring (Species Accumulation Curve) discussed in the study  . The architecture demonstrates how a low-code platform can effectively eliminate manual transcription bottlenecks while maintaining taxonomic precision and data integrit.
